title: Air-fryer haddock fillet
subtitle: Delicate white fish with butter, cooked simply
excerpt: Haddock fillets brushed with melted butter, seasoned, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes. Haddock has a slightly sweeter flavour than cod with finer flakes. The butter provides the richness the lean fish needs during cooking.
sourceNotes: Haddock is the second most popular fish in British fish and chips, preferred over cod in northern England and Scotland. Fresh haddock has a slightly sweeter, more pronounced flavour than cod with finer-grained flesh. Smoked haddock is a separate product used for kedgeree and cullen skink; this recipe uses fresh unsmoked fillet.

content.text: If the haddock has skin, leave it on during cooking. The skin protects the delicate flesh from the direct heat of the basket and keeps moisture inside the fillet. It lifts off in one piece after cooking and does not need to be eaten.

Pat the {{haddock-fillet}} fillets dry with kitchen paper. Brush all over with melted {{unsalted-butter}} and season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Place the fillets skin-side down in the basket. Cook at 200°C for 10 minutes for thinner pieces or 12 minutes for thicker. The haddock is done when the flesh is fully opaque and flakes easily when pressed at the thickest point. Rest for 1 minute before serving with {{lemon}}.

items.fix: Haddock has less fat than salmon and dries out quickly past the point of doneness. Check at 10 minutes. The butter or oil is not optional for haddock: it is the main source of moisture protection during cooking
A light spray of oil on the basket prevents sticking. The skin should peel cleanly from the basket once fully cooked. If it is still adhering, give it another 30 seconds before trying to lift
items.cause: Basket not oiled or not preheated
items.symptom: Skin stuck to the basket after cooking

items.fix: Use a wide flat spatula and support the full length of the fillet. Haddock's fine grain breaks more easily than cod if handled roughly or cooked past the point of doneness
items.symptom: Fillet breaking apart when transferred to the plate

content.text: Air-fryer haddock is the everyday fish dinner in northern England and Scotland where it is preferred over cod. Serve with chips and mushy peas, with new potatoes and a butter sauce, or with a green salad. The butter finish gives the flesh a richness that plain oil does not, and the shorter cook time compared to oven baking keeps it moist.
